business.type_label: creative studio for interior & garden design (with workshops + online shop)

engine.ladder_reasoning: Studio sells defined design expertise (garden design, interior design, styling) fronted by named people (Mark & Vivian) and an explicit 3-tier process ('Onze werkwijze'); trusting the firm's craft is the decision, so trust wins the ladder before work/place.

goal.one_action: Book a workshop / design session, or contact the studio (secondary: buy shop products, subscribe newsletter)

structure.archetype_note: Hybrid: services studio + bookable workshops (calendly) + transactional online store (cart/checkout) + blog + contact/newsletter — multiple distinct conversion paths, not one action.

mapped.observed_as: Ritme van het Land | workshopreeks (tiered pricing)
Schrijf je nu in (newsletter) + Pinterest/Instagram links

unmapped.observed: WINKEL product catalog with category filters + cart/checkout
unmapped.why_no_fit: Canonical sections have no transactional storefront/catalog-with-checkout section; 'pricing' understates a browsable multi-category shop with cart
Blog index isn't a canonical section id

proposed_new.name: external-booking-redirect
proposed_new.evidence: All workshop/session/brainstorm/walk CTAs ('Boek hier een workshop of brainstorm', 'boek een kruiden wandeling', 'Boek hier jou sessie') hand off to Calendly; no on-site scheduling.

proposed_new.name: storefront-checkout
proposed_new.evidence: WINKEL is a real transactional store: commerce.cart_or_checkout=true, category filters (ceramics, furniture, lighting, outdoor…), and ~15 fixed product prices (€39.50–€2,090.00) with on-site purchase — beyond a browse-only catalog and not the recurring membership-checkout.

confidence.why: fetch_quality=rendered (not full) — capped at medium per rules; contact form only surfaced via render pass, and portfolio/blog counts are unresolved (portfolio 'being photographed', blog post_count null).

gap_findings: Multi-job site (services + bookable workshops + transactional store + blog + newsletter) has no single conversion action — the 5-engine model can only name one; real business is a hybrid the taxonomy can't hold whole.
A full on-site product store with cart/checkout has no canonical capability (closed vocab 'catalog' = browse-only; 'membership-checkout' = recurring) — proposed storefront-checkout.
No canonical section for a transactional storefront/catalog block or a blog index.
